question: Looking at the US unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The highlighted states all have highly diverse unemployment rate levels.
options.B: The unemployment rate gap between Colorado and South Dakota might exceed that between New York and Kentucky.
options.C: Colorado and South Dakota have similar unemployment rates.
options.D: The unemployment rate differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: Colorado (7.1%) and South Dakota (8.7%) have a 1.6% unemployment rate difference, while New York (8.8%) and Kentucky (9.4%) have only a 0.6% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Colorado and South Dakota into the same color bin (7-9% range), making their large difference appear similar, while New York and Kentucky appear more different due to different color bins.
